title: rubus hystrix subsp. rufescens
intro: Rubus hystrix subsp. rufescens, also known as the bristly blackberry, is a species of thorny shrub in the rose family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its astringent and anti-inflammatory effects, which are attributed to its high content of phenolic compounds and flavonoids. The bristly blackberry has been associated with various health benefits, including its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of Rubus hystrix subsp. rufescens can be found in various forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of gastrointestinal disorders.
